comment: GO M3 — declarative precompile manifest. Each listed .hexa script is compiled by tool/build_precompile.hexa into release/precompile/hexa_run.<key> where <key> = sha256(source).substring(0,16) + '_' + version_str() (must match self/main.hexa cmd_run cache key format). On `hexa run <script>` cmd_run probes release/precompile/ first → cache HIT skips clang fork (cold-cache fork-storm mitigation, internal axis). GO M6 (this commit): expanded from 2 demo entries to 10 production-hot scripts. Selection criteria — high git churn (>= 2 commits / 3 months) · user-fired routinely (INBOX / GO log) · deterministic source (no mono_ns / random / mtime in source content) · CLI-facing.

descriptions.tool/build_hexa_cli.hexa: core CLI bootstrap — `hexa` driver build entry. Original M3 demo entry. Fired on every fresh-checkout dev cycle.
descriptions.tool/atlas_cli.hexa: atlas SSOT verbs (hash · stats · lookup · register · dump · export). Original M3 demo. 14 commits in last 3 months — top-2 churn in tool/. User-fired routinely via /atlas slash command and `hexa atlas` driver shim.
descriptions.tool/verify_cli.hexa: atlas verifier — `hexa verify <id>|--expr|--fence|rubric|list`. 14 commits in last 3 months — tied for top-1 churn. User-fired routinely via /verify slash command + INBOX verify-arm extension cycle.
descriptions.tool/inbox_sync.hexa: INBOX/PATCHES.yaml well-formedness verifier. 2 commits / 3 months. User-fired weekly during inbox sweep cycles.
descriptions.tool/inbox_promote.hexa: INBOX matured-entry archiver (applied -> archived). 2 commits / 3 months. Companion to inbox_sync, fired alongside.
descriptions.tool/cli_wrappers.hexa: ~/.hx/bin/* wrapper SSOT installer (drift detect + auto-write). 2 commits / 3 months. Fired on every wrapper drift report.
descriptions.tool/audit_forbidden_exts.hexa: FIRMWARE.md G-T0 banned-source-extension audit. 2 commits / 3 months. Fired by .githooks/pre-commit AND CI bootstrap.
descriptions.tool/install_firmware_hook.hexa: FIRMWARE.md G-T3 pre-push hook installer. Fired once per fresh checkout — fits 'sub-second first-call' UX goal exactly.
descriptions.tool/build_hexat_linux.hexa: cross-build hexat transpiler for linux/x86_64 from macOS. Fired by anima CLM dispatch and release-prep. (Renamed from build_hexa_v2_linux.hexa in #1221; manifest ref was stale → Stage-3 'manifest missing on disk'.)
descriptions.tool/build_precompile.hexa: self — the precompile builder itself. Fired by release CI to populate release/precompile/. Including in manifest means subsequent CI runs hit cache (bootstrap acceleration).

schema_notes: M6 schema extension (additive only — `tool/build_precompile.hexa::_parse_scripts` only reads the `"scripts"` array, ignoring everything else). `descriptions` = per-entry human reasoning (1-line per script, mirrors `scripts` keys). `categories` = coarse bucket (build/atlas/verify/inbox/install/audit) for future filtering verbs (e.g. `--category=atlas`). Both fields are documentation-only today; future M7+ may surface them via `--list --verbose` once a richer parser lands. Adding/removing scripts: update `scripts` (load-bearing) AND mirror the entry in `descriptions` + `categories` (lint-only).
